title: Google Trends Retail Attention Surge Detector
summary: Use the pytrends library (free, unofficial Google Trends API) to pull weekly Google search volume for a stock's ticker symbol and company name. Compute a normalized attention score: (current_week_volume - 52W_avg) / 52W_std. A spike > 2 standard deviations above the 52-week average indicates a surge in retail attention that historically precedes short-term price momentum (documented in multiple academic studies). Add as an optional signal boost in signals.py for momentum-regime stocks (H > 0.55). Rate-limit to one API call per ticker per day to avoid pytrends throttling.
research_topic: Win Rate Improvement — Portfolio Construction, Trade Selectivity & Alternative Data
expected_impact: +2–4% win rate for retail-driven momentum setups
